title: Migrate 76 rows to pbi events; docs/backlog.md becomes the generated view; uniqueness retargeted
action: Per CONTEXT v2 D5. scripts/migrate_backlog_pbis.mjs: status-anchored parse of the legacy table (NEVER positional — Story/CoS contain literal '|'; anchor on the status cell like classify-lane's parseRow), assert exactly 76 unique P-ids max P77; normalize decorated statuses (P8/P13/P32: strip to enum, preserve the decoration text into the item's cos/notes via the add event — nothing dropped); append one pbi add event per row (id preserved via the migration-only --id path or the lib call), plus a status event where needed; idempotent (re-run detects existing pbi events for those ids => no-op) and refuses divergence. RUN it in this cell. Then bee backlog render --write => generated docs/backlog.md committed. Retarget scripts/backlog_uniqueness.mjs (same path/name — test_verify_manifest pins it at :117 and the --check arg-variant at :187): fold invariants (unique ids, valid enum, every kind:pbi line parseable) + render --check freshness; green pre- and post-migration. impact_registry --write (new script file).

truths: 76 ids migrated, none renumbered, decorated status prose preserved
generated view committed; render --check green; view is short (done/declined links only)
uniqueness suite same path, still manifest-pinned, green

verify: node scripts/backlog_uniqueness.mjs --check && node .bee/bin/bee.mjs backlog render --check && node scripts/impact_registry.mjs --check
